meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to build a high-impact portfolio. A beginner-friendly guide for student developers.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping high-fidelity, functional applications, not static homework. By pairing the Antigravity project template with Claude Code, you can scaffold, engineer, and deploy a professional-grade SaaS project in hours. This workflow demonstrates technical mastery and real-world problem-solving to potential hiring managers instantly.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the cursor blinking in a void—the silent, agonizing reminder of an empty GitHub profile. You have the degree, the theory, and the passion, but the industry doesn't care about your GPA; they care about what you can ship. The gap between 'learning to code' and 'becoming a developer' isn't measured in hours, but in deployed projects that solve actual user problems.

Stop building endless to-do lists and start architecting solutions that mirror production-grade environments. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you bypass the boilerplate fatigue that kills student momentum. Combined with the surgical precision of Claude Code, you are no longer just writing syntax; you are orchestrating an entire application ecosystem from the command line.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Establish a production-ready directory structure that includes CI/CD hooks and environment management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the standard Antigravity web-app template with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase integration configured.

steps.title: Define the Core Data Schema
steps.description: Create a robust database schema that handles user authentication and relational data, ensuring your app has a backend heartbeat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Supabase migration file for a User table and a Project table, including foreign key relationships and row-level security policies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Create SQL migration for user-project relation'

steps.title: Implement Modular UI Components
steps.description: Build high-conversion UI components that look like professional SaaS products, not student prototypes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout using the Antigravity component library, including a sidebar navigation and a data-table view.

steps.title: Integrate Business Logic
steps.description: Connect your frontend to the database layer to make your application interactive and dynamic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service function to handle CRUD operations for the Project model using the Supabase client, with error handling and loading states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement CRUD services in src/services/project.ts'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Push your code to a live environment, turning your local project into a shareable portfolio link.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline for Vercel, ensuring all environment variables are mapped correctly from the Antigravity config.

pro_tips: Always commit your work with descriptive messages referencing the specific feature you just built; it shows employers your iterative development process.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your data services; testing is the number one differentiator between a junior and a mid-level developer.
Keep your README.md updated with a 'Why I built this' section; context is more important than code to a recruiter.
Focus on one 'hero' feature that solves a specific user pain point rather than bloating the app with unnecessary functionality.
Audit your site performance using Lighthouse after every major push to demonstrate your commitment to user experience.
content.next_steps: After completing your first project, document the architecture in a blog post, add the live link to your LinkedIn bio, and begin contributing to an open-source project to scale your impact.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to transform from a student into a builder. Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
